2605.29795.title: MEMENTO: Leveraging Web as a Learning Signal for Low-Data Domains
2605.29795.paper_summary: Real-world tasks often lack large labeled datasets, motivating extensive work on learning in low-data regimes. However, existing approaches such as few-shot prompting, instruction tuning, and synthetic data generation, continue to treat labeled or pseudo-labeled data as the primary learning signal. In contrast, human practitioners acquire expertise through repeated, self-directed interaction with the open web, progressively refining both domain knowledge and search strategies. We propose MEMENTO, a framework that treats the web as a learning signal rather than a stateless retrieval interface. MEMENTO operates at two levels: within each session, it conducts iterative web exploration via an Adaptive Exploration Tree (AET) that decomposes tasks into evolving questions and reflects on intermediate findings; across sessions, it accumulates experience through dual-channel memory, separating declarative knowledge (facts) from procedural knowledge (search strategies). This design enables agents to learn reusable research strategies and domain expertise from trajectories of web interaction without additional model training. We evaluate MEMENTO on two low-data professional domains: sales automation and legal research. Our empirical results show consistent improvements in performance over ReAct based baselines (+25.6% on sales automation and 36.5% on legal research), demonstrating that the web can serve as a scalable learning source for acquiring task-specific expertise in data-scarce settings.
2605.29795.paper_summary_zh: 現實世界的任務通常缺乏大量標記的數據集，這促使了在低數據環境下學習的廣泛研究。然而，現有的方法如少量提示、指令調整和合成數據生成，仍然將標記或偽標記數據視為主要的學習信號。相對而言，人類實踐者通過與開放網絡的反覆自我導向互動來獲得專業知識，逐步完善領域知識和搜索策略。我們提出了MEMENTO，一個將網絡視為學習信號而非無狀態檢索介面的框架。MEMENTO在兩個層面上運作：在每個會話內，它通過自適應探索樹（AET）進行迭代的網絡探索，將任務分解為不斷演變的問題並反思中間發現；在會話之間，它通過雙通道記憶累積經驗，將陳述性知識（事實）與程序性知識（搜索策略）分開。這一設計使代理能夠從網絡互動的軌跡中學習可重用的研究策略和領域專業知識，而無需額外的模型訓練。我們在兩個低數據專業領域對MEMENTO進行了評估：銷售自動化和法律研究。我們的實證結果顯示，與基於ReAct的基準相比，性能有一致的提升（銷售自動化提升25.6%，法律研究提升36.5%），證明網絡可以作為一個可擴展的學習來源，用於在數據稀缺的環境中獲取任務特定的專業知識。
2605.29795.author: Ashutosh Ojha et.al.
2605.29795.authors: Ashutosh Ojha, Vinay Aggarwal, Ashutosh Srivastava, Siddharth Yedlapati, Yaman K Singla, Jitendra Ajmera
